workflow.name: Track Telegram expenses with GPT-4 and Google Sheets (self-learning categories)
workflow.description:
**💸 HOW IT WORKS — AI TELEGRAM EXPENSE TRACKER**

This workflow transforms natural Telegram messages into structured expenses using AI — without forms, manual typing, or complex inputs.

Simply send a message like:


*Groceries 23€ yesterday*



The workflow validates the sender, understands the intent, extracts structured data, and prepares the expense for approval before saving.

────────────────

**🔄 WORKFLOW OVERVIEW**

**🟩 1. Secure Input Layer**
Incoming Telegram messages are checked against a list of approved Chat IDs to ensure only authorized users can create expenses.

**🟦 2. AI Expense Detection**
An AI layer analyzes the message and decides whether it represents a real financial transaction.
Non-expense messages are safely ignored to avoid noise in your data.

**🟨 3. Smart Category Intelligence**
Existing categories are loaded from Google Sheets and compared with the message content.
If no suitable category exists, the workflow can suggest and learn new categories over time.

**🟪 4. Structured Data Extraction**
AI converts natural language into structured fields:

date

amount

category

description

shared vs personal expense

Supports German and English input.

**🟥 5. Human Approval & Storage**
Before saving, the user confirms the extracted result directly via Telegram.
After approval, the expense is appended to Google Sheets automatically.

────────────────

📋 SETUP REQUIREMENTS

Before using this workflow, make sure the following components are ready:

1️⃣**Telegram Bot**
Create a Telegram bot using BotFather and connect it to the Telegram Trigger node in n8n. Detailed setup instructions can be found [here](https://medium.com/@robinvm/how-to-create-a-telegram-bot-and-connect-it-to-n8n-99eec8ad6ed0).

2️⃣**LLM API Access**
An API Key for a Large Language Model (LLM) is required for:

expense detection

category matching

structured data extraction

Add your API credentials inside the AI node configuration.

3️⃣**Google Sheets**

Create two Google Sheets before importing the workflow.

***EXPENSES***
Required columns:
*date, amount, category, description, common_expense, Person*

***EXPENSE_CATEGORIES***
Required columns:
*category, description, examples*

The workflow reads existing data and appends new entries automatically.

────────────────

💡**KEY FEATURES**

• AI-powered expense detection from natural language
• Self-learning category system
• Human-in-the-loop approval step
• Multi-language support (DE & EN)
• Clean Google Sheets integration
• Designed for real-life shared finance tracking

────────────────

👥**MULTI-USER SUPPORT**

Built for couples, roommates, or teams.

Add multiple Chat IDs in:
Security — Allow Approved Chat IDs

Each expense is automatically tagged with the sender.
Shared expenses are stored as true in the common_expense column, while personal expenses default to false unless shared spending is detected.

This allows easy downstream analysis, dashboards, or automation.

values.content:
=You are an EXPENSE EXTRACTION assistant.

Your task:
Analyze a chat message and decide whether it contains an EXPENSE.

---

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)

Return ONLY one JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
"is_expense": boolean,
}

Never add explanations.
Never add additional fields.
----------------------------

DEFINITION OF AN EXPENSE

An expense MUST include:

* a description (what money was spent on)
* a numeric amount

If one of these is missing → it is NOT an expense.

---

FIELD RULES

is_expense

* true if message clearly contains a payment or spending
* otherwise false


---

LANGUAGE

Messages may be German or English.
Extract meaning naturally.

---




PROCESSING LOGIC

1. First decide if it is an expense.
2. If NOT an expense, return:

{
"is_expense": false
}

3. If it IS an expense, return:

{
"is_expense": false
}

---

CHAT MESSAGE:
{{ $json.message.text }}

You are a strict classification engine.

You receive category lines in this format:
[Category] | Description | Examples

Task:
1) Check if the message belongs to ANY existing category.
2) If YES → return match:true.
3) If NO → propose ONE new category.

Rules:
- Be concise.
- Use similar style as existing categories.
- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- No markdown.
- No explanations.

Output schema:
{
  "match": boolean,
  "suggested_category": string | null,
  "description": string | null,
  "examples": string | null
}

nodes.name: Telegram — Ask To Create New Category

parameters.message: =📝 Please fill out the form for the new category

values.content:
=You are an EXPENSE EXTRACTION AGENT.

Your task:
Analyze a chat message and extract expense data.

---

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY)

Return ONLY one JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
"date": string,
"amount": float,
"category": string,
"description": string,
"common_expense": boolean
}

Never add explanations.
Never add additional fields.
----------------------------

FIELD RULES

date

* Extract date only if mentioned
* Format: YYYY-MM-DD
* If missing AND is_expense = true → use TODAY_DATE
* If is_expense = false → ""

amount

* Numeric only
* No currency symbols
* If is_expense = false → 0

description

* Short, clean object or service
* NO numbers
* NO date words
* Examples: "Pizza", "Taxi ride", "Groceries"
* If is_expense = false → ""

category

* Choose ONE category from AVAILABLE_CATEGORIES
* If none fits → create a NEW short category label
* If is_expense = false → ""

common_expense

* true ONLY if shared spending is implied
  ("wir", "geteilt", "split", "shared", "für uns")
* otherwise false

---

LANGUAGE

Messages may be German or English.
Extract meaning naturally.

---

INPUT VARIABLES

TODAY_DATE = {{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}

AVAILABLE_CATEGORIES:
{{ $json.category_prompt }}

---

PROCESSING LOGIC

1. Decide if the message is an expense.
2. If NOT an expense, return:

{
"is_expense": false,
"date": "",
"amount": 0,
"category": "",
"description": "",
"common_expense": false
}

3. If it IS an expense:
   Fill all fields according to rules.

---

CHAT MESSAGE:
{{ $node["Security — Allow Approved Chat IDs"].json.message.text }}

parameters.content:
## HOW IT WORKS — AI TELEGRAM EXPENSE TRACKER

This workflow automatically converts Telegram messages into structured expenses using AI.

Instead of filling out forms, simply send a natural message like:

Groceries 23€ yesterday

The workflow analyzes, validates, and stores your expense step by step.

────────────────

🔄 **WORKFLOW OVERVIEW**

🟩 **Input & Security**
A Telegram message is received and validated against approved Chat IDs.

🟦 **AI Detection Layer**
AI checks whether the message contains a real expense.
Non-financial messages are ignored.

🟨 **Category Intelligence**
Existing categories are loaded and compared.
If no match is found, the workflow can suggest and learn new categories.

🟪 **Expense Extraction**
AI extracts structured data such as date, amount, category, and description.

🟥 **Approval & Save**
You confirm the result before it is saved to Google Sheets.

────────────────

📋 **WHAT YOU NEED**

Create two Google Sheets before using this workflow.

EXPENSES
Columns:
date, amount, category, description, common_expense, Person

EXPENSE_CATEGORIES
Columns:
category, description, examples

The workflow will read and append data automatically.


────────────────

💡 **KEY FEATURES**

• AI-powered expense detection
• Self-learning category system
• Human approval before saving
• Works with natural language (German & English)

────────────────

⭐ **MULTI-USER HINT**

This workflow is designed for multiple people.

You can add several Chat IDs in the
Security — Allow Approved Chat IDs node
so different users can track expenses at the same time.

Each entry is tagged with the person who sent the message.

Expenses can be marked as shared or personal.
Shared expenses are stored in the common_expense column as a boolean value (true/false).

By default, expenses are treated as personal unless shared spending is detected.

## INPUT
When a new Telegram message is received, the workflow first performs a **security validation**.
Only messages from approved Telegram users are allowed to continue.

This prevents unauthorized users from triggering your expense automation.



⚠️ **Action Required**
-> Add your Telegram Credentials to the following Nodes:
   **Receive Message**
-> Replace Telegram Chat IDs in following Security Nodes:
**Allow Approved Chat IDs**

## AI DETECTION LAYER
This section uses AI to determine whether an incoming Telegram message actually contains an expense.

Instead of processing every message, the workflow first performs a lightweight classification step to reduce errors and unnecessary processing.

**1. AI — Detect Expense Message**
The AI analyzes the text and decides if it represents a real expense.

**2. Parse — Expense Detection Output**
The AI response is converted into structured JSON data.

**3. Filter — Only Continue If Expense**
The workflow only continues if is_expense = true.


⚠️ **Action Required**

--> Add your AI Credentials to the following Node:
   **Detect Expense Message**

## CATEGORY INTELLIGENCE - Smart Category Handling
This section ensures that every expense is assigned to the correct category.

First, the workflow loads your existing categories from Google Sheets and formats them into an AI-readable structure.
The AI then checks whether the new expense fits an existing category.

If no match is found, the workflow proposes a new category and asks for confirmation before adding it to your category list.



🔎 **What happens here?**
-> Load existing categories from Sheets
-> AI compares the message against known categories
-> If no match is found, AI suggests a new category (name, description, examples)
-> You can approve the suggestion or create your own category via form
-> New categories are added to your system and used automatically in future expenses





⚠️ **Action Required**
-> Connect to the Sheet that contains the category data in following Sheet Nodes: 
     **Load Existing Categories, Add Suggested Category, Add Edited Category**

-> Add your Telegram Credentials to the following Nodes:
   **Ask To Create New Category,  Edit New Category Form, Category Created Confirmation**

--> Add your AI Credentials to the following Node:
   **Classify Expense Category**

## EXPENSE EXTRACTION
In this section, the workflow uses AI to transform the detected expense message into structured data.

The AI receives the original chat message together with the current category list and extracts all relevant expense information in a strict JSON format.

🔎 **What happens here?**
-> Merge message data with available categories
-> AI extracts structured fields (date, amount, category, description, shared expense)
-> Output is converted into clean JSON for further processing
-> This step converts natural language into database-ready expense records.


⚠️ **Action Required**
--> Add your AI Credentials to the following Node:
   **Extract Structured Expense Data**

## APPROVAL & SAVE

Before saving the expense, the workflow asks the user for final confirmation via Telegram.
This ensures that extracted data is correct and prevents incorrect entries from being stored.

Only approved expenses are written to Google Sheets and confirmed back to the user.


🔎 **What happens here?**
-> Show extracted expense data for review
-> Wait for user approval
-> Add user information to the record
-> Save the expense to Google Sheets
-> Send a confirmation message

This step adds a human-in-the-loop safety layer before data is stored permanently.





⚠️ **Action Required**
-> Connect to the Sheet that contains the category data in following Sheet Nodes: 
     **Save Expense**

-> Add your Telegram Credentials to the following Nodes:
   **Confirm Expense Before Save, Expense Saved Confirmation**

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
